summary: Replayed the rebased `0.177.39` Gitea stack and self-hosted Actions runner from branch head `a3f805e6`, recovered a transient Keycloak/PostgreSQL admin API interruption during the first attempt, then completed a clean rerun and preserved the earlier successful private Git push gate plus branch workflow evidence on runner docker-build-lv3.

verification.check: Focused Gitea runner repo slice and branch validation surfaces
verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml python -m pytest -q tests/test_gitea_runtime_role.py` passed with `8 passed in 0.18s`; `./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards` passed; `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python3 scripts/validate_repository_data_models.py --validate` passed; `python3 scripts/generate_diagrams.py --check` passed after refreshing `docs/diagrams/agent-coordination-map.excalidraw`; and `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python3 scripts/platform_manifest.py --check` passed after rewriting `build/platform-manifest.json`.

`make converge-gitea` completed successfully from rebased branch head `a3f805e6` with `proxmox_florin ok=36 changed=4 failed=0`, `postgres-lv3 ok=24 changed=0 failed=0`, `docker-runtime-lv3 ok=224 changed=1 failed=0`, and `docker-build-lv3 ok=100 changed=5 failed=0`.

verification.check: Transient Keycloak admin recovery during rebased replay
verification.observed: The first rebased replay attempt failed in `lv3.platform.keycloak_runtime` while creating the operations portal OAuth client because the local token endpoint returned `HTTP 500`. Keycloak logs on `docker-runtime-lv3` showed PostgreSQL broken-pipe and terminated-connection errors, a direct admin token probe immediately returned `HTTP 200`, and the next full replay completed cleanly without changing repo automation.

The Gitea admin API `GET /api/v1/admin/actions/runners` returned runner `1` named `docker-build-lv3` with `status: online`, `busy: false`, and labels `self-hosted`, `linux`, `amd64`, and `docker`; direct host-side inspection on `docker-build-lv3` also confirmed container `lv3-gitea-runner` running.

A branch push to `http://100.64.0.1:3009/ops/proxmox_florin_server.git` using the mirrored `ops-gitea` admin token over HTTP basic auth was accepted only after the server-side gate passed `alert-rule-validation`, `ansible-lint`, `ansible-syntax`, `artifact-secret-scan`, `dependency-graph`, `integration-tests`, `packer-validate`, `schema-validation`, `security-scan`, `service-completeness`, `tofu-validate`, `type-check`, and `yaml-lint`.

Gitea recorded workflow run `20` for `head_branch: codex/ws-0229-live-apply` and `head_sha: b83d26083261b05782e9ccde2abf78f6d232d20c`; the run finished with `status: completed` and `conclusion: success`. Job `20` named `validate` ran on `runner_id: 1` / `runner_name: docker-build-lv3`, started at `2026-03-28T16:21:39Z`, and completed at `2026-03-28T16:21:42Z`.

The first branch push attempt intentionally exercised the gate failure path and exposed two branch-local issues: `workstreams.yaml` used invalid status `in-progress`, and the generated coordination diagram was stale after claiming `ws-0229-live-apply`. After fixing both, rerunning `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/validate_repository_data_models.py --validate` and `python3 scripts/generate_diagrams.py --check` passed, and the second push was accepted.

notes: ADR 0229 is marked implemented retroactively because the first repo and platform versions where the runner capability became true were already recorded by ADR 0143 (`0.165.0` / `0.130.15` on `2026-03-26`); this receipt records the fresh rebased replay and branch-level automation proof from `0.177.39` / `0.130.38`.
The private Gitea git SSH endpoint on port `2222` did not accept the Proxmox host bootstrap key; the documented smoke push used the mirrored `ops-gitea` admin token over HTTP basic auth instead.

This branch intentionally does not update `README.md`, `VERSION`, `changelog.md`, or `versions/stack.yaml` yet because the integrated platform truth already described the live runner capability before the branch-local verification replay.
